name: Business Requirements Document
description: Template for capturing the business context, goals, stakeholders, scope, rules, constraints, and success criteria for a project.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.description: A concise summary of the business problem, proposed initiative, and intended outcome.
guidance: Explain the business context in plain language.
State why this initiative matters now.
Summarize the expected value of the project.

sections.title: Business Problem Statement
sections.description: Defines the current business problem, pain point, or opportunity being addressed.
guidance: Describe the current issue or missed opportunity.
Explain who is affected and how.
State the cost or impact of not solving it.

sections.title: Business Objectives
sections.description: Lists the measurable business goals this initiative is expected to achieve.
guidance: Use measurable business outcomes where possible.
Separate primary objectives from secondary ones.
Align objectives to organizational priorities.

sections.title: Stakeholders
sections.description: Identifies the key business, product, delivery, and technical stakeholders involved.
guidance: List stakeholder groups and named roles where available.
Clarify ownership, influence, and approval responsibility.
Capture who benefits from or is impacted by the project.

sections.title: Business Context
sections.description: Explains the wider business environment, process, market, or operational background relevant to the initiative.
guidance: Provide enough context for a new team member to understand the domain.
Mention existing workflows or legacy systems if relevant.
Highlight current limitations or dependencies.

sections.title: Scope
sections.description: Defines what is included within the boundaries of this initiative.
guidance: State what business areas, processes, or capabilities are in scope.
Be concrete rather than vague.
Keep scope aligned to the stated objectives.

sections.title: Out of Scope
sections.description: Defines what is explicitly excluded from the initiative to avoid ambiguity.
guidance: List exclusions clearly.
Call out nearby items people may mistakenly assume are included.
Use this section to reduce expectation drift.

sections.title: Business Rules
sections.description: Captures domain rules, policies, and operational constraints that the solution must respect.
guidance: Document rules that affect behavior, approvals, calculations, compliance, or workflow.
Differentiate permanent rules from temporary workarounds.
Call out exceptions where relevant.

sections.title: Assumptions
sections.description: Lists assumptions made during planning and document creation.
guidance: Only include assumptions that materially affect planning or outcomes.
Write them so they can later be validated or disproven.
Avoid mixing assumptions with confirmed facts.

sections.title: Constraints
sections.description: Defines business, timeline, budget, legal, operational, or organizational constraints.
guidance: Capture limits that the solution must operate within.
Mention deadlines, regulatory obligations, budget ceilings, or staffing realities.
Explain why the constraint matters.

sections.title: Risks
sections.description: Summarizes major business and delivery risks known at this stage.
guidance: Focus on material risks, not speculative noise.
Include dependency, adoption, resourcing, and operational risks.
Where possible, note mitigation direction.

sections.title: Success Criteria
sections.description: Defines how the business will determine whether the initiative succeeded.
guidance: Link success criteria back to business objectives.
Prefer measurable targets where possible.
Include qualitative outcomes when quantitative measures are not yet defined.

sections.title: High Level Timeline
sections.description: Provides a business-facing timeline view of the initiative.
guidance: Keep this section high level.
Use major milestones rather than detailed sprint plans.
Highlight externally important dates if known.

sections.title: Approval and Sign-off
sections.description: Captures who approves the business requirements and any sign-off status.
guidance: List approving roles or named approvers if known.
Record sign-off status or pending approvals.
Do not invent approval states.

validationRules: Business objectives must be consistent with the problem statement.
Scope must not contradict out_of_scope items.
Success criteria should map to at least one business objective where possible.
Constraints must not be written as objectives.
Assumptions must be distinguishable from confirmed facts.

promptHints.systemIntent: Generate a business-focused BRD that is clear, structured, and useful for both leadership and delivery teams.
mustDo: Write in business language first, not implementation language.
State unknowns clearly rather than guessing.
Preserve distinctions between confirmed facts, inferred context, and assumptions.
Keep the document suitable as an upstream input to PRD and FRD generation.
mustNotDo: Do not invent KPIs, budgets, dates, or stakeholders.
Do not drift into detailed technical design.
Do not restate the same point across multiple sections unless required for clarity.
